title: Small LLMs, Big Finds: Automated Vulnerability Discovery

modules.title: Why Small LLMs Are Becoming Preferred Tools for Offensive Security Automation
modules.sub: Exploring why compact models outpace larger alternatives in offensive workflows
modules.description: Learners examine the operational and technical reasons small LLMs have gained traction in offensive security, from air-gapped deployment to inference speed. They compare model size trade-offs against capability requirements for vulnerability discovery tasks and assess real-world attacker use cases that have emerged in the wild.

modules.title: Code Analysis Pipelines: How LLMs Identify Bug Classes at Scale
modules.sub: Building automated pipelines that surface known vulnerability patterns in code
modules.description: Learners construct end-to-end pipelines that feed source code, firmware, and API definitions into an LLM for automated bug-class detection. They map common vulnerability categories — such as memory corruption, injection flaws, and logic errors — to prompting strategies that reliably surface them at scale.

modules.title: Fine-Tuning and Prompt Engineering for Vulnerability-Specific Tasks
modules.sub: Shaping model behavior to focus on security-relevant code patterns
modules.description: Learners apply targeted prompt engineering techniques and explore lightweight fine-tuning approaches to improve a model's precision on vulnerability-specific tasks. They design and test prompt templates against benchmark datasets, measuring how instruction framing and few-shot examples shift detection rates for specific bug classes.

modules.title: Evaluating LLM-Generated Findings: False Positives, Severity Triage, and Chain-of-Thought Auditing
modules.sub: Critically assessing model output quality, accuracy, and reasoning transparency
modules.description: Learners develop evaluation frameworks to measure false positive rates, score severity assignments, and audit the chain-of-thought reasoning behind LLM findings. They practice triage workflows that separate actionable findings from noise and identify systematic biases introduced by training data or prompt design.

modules.title: Defensive Inversion: Using the Same Pipelines for Secure Code Review
modules.sub: Repurposing offensive scanning tooling to strengthen developer security practices
modules.description: Learners reorient the vulnerability discovery pipelines built earlier as developer-facing secure code review tools embedded in the SDLC. They adapt prompts, output formats, and severity thresholds for a defensive audience and evaluate how the same model behavior that aids attackers can be constrained and redirected to aid defenders.

modules.title: Building and Hardening LLM-Assisted SAST/DAST Workflows
modules.sub: Integrating and securing LLM components within existing security testing infrastructure
modules.description: Learners integrate LLM-assisted analysis into static and dynamic application security testing workflows, connecting model output to existing ticketing and remediation pipelines. They also harden the LLM components themselves against prompt injection, data leakage, and adversarial manipulation that could subvert the scanning infrastructure.

modules.title: Governance, Responsible Disclosure, and Legal Boundaries of AI-Driven Scanning
modules.sub: Navigating ethical, legal, and organizational responsibilities when deploying AI scanners
modules.description: Learners examine the legal frameworks, organizational policies, and responsible disclosure norms that govern the use of AI-driven vulnerability discovery tools. They work through scenario-based decisions covering authorization boundaries, handling of discovered vulnerabilities, and the documentation practices required to defend deployment choices to stakeholders and regulators.

synopsis: Compact, locally-runnable language models are enabling attackers — and defenders — to scan codebases, firmware, and APIs for vulnerabilities at a speed and scale no human team can match. Security engineers who understand how to build, evaluate, and constrain these pipelines will determine whether this capability becomes an advantage or a liability.
rationale: Accessible, fine-tunable small LLMs are democratizing automated exploit discovery, fundamentally shifting the economics of both offensive research and secure development practice.
learning_outcome: Learners can construct an LLM-assisted vulnerability discovery pipeline, critically evaluate its output for accuracy and bias, and repurpose the same tooling defensively within a secure SDLC.
source_signals: Small models also found the vulnerabilities that Mythos found
AI cybersecurity is not proof of work
